4250092 - North Park/Mid-City Bikeways: University Bikeway Addendum No: 5 (Published)
Notice
[Closed Bid]
Project Number 4250092
Title North Park/Mid-City Bikeways: University Bikeway
Basic Information
Reference Number 0000360376
Issuing Organization SANDAG
Owner Organization SANDAG
Project Type IFB - Invitation for Bid (Formal)
Project Number 4250092
Title North Park/Mid-City Bikeways: University Bikeway
Source ID PU.AG.USA.1309051.C13814151
Details
Location San Diego County, California
Description The San Diego Association of Governments (SANDAG) is seeking
contractors to deliver the North Park/Mid-City Bikeways: University
Bikeway project. This project is constructing approximately 2.8 miles of
Class II and Class IV bikeway along University Avenue between Estella
Avenue and 70th Street within the City of San Diego and the City of La
Mesa and approximately 0.25 miles of Bike Boulevard improvements on
Estrella Avenue between Orange Avenue and University Avenue.
Roadway improvements are required to accommodate the bikeway.
Features include raised concrete medians, concrete curb and gutter,
asphalt, concrete dike, bus islands, a protected intersection, street lighting,
traffic signal modifications, and other traffic safety measures linked to
signage and striping.
Work includes, but is not limited to, controlling for water pollution; traffic
control; erosion control; clearing and grubbing; curb, gutter, cross gutter,
driveway, colored concrete, and sidewalk work; roadway excavation;
imported borrow; asphalt concrete pavement; PCC pavement; landscaping
and irrigation; proprietary BMPs; wayfinding signage; pavement markings
and signage; street lighting; trenching and backfill for underground electric
conduits; water appurtenances and fire hydrant relocations; storm drain
installation; traffic signal modifications. Additive alternate A consists of
pavement rehabilitation and associated curb ramps, traffic signal
modifications and other appurtenant work associated with rehabilitating
the roadway along University Avenue.
The work will also include the procurement of all materials and performing
all other work necessary to complete the work in accordance with the
project plans and specifications.
This project is federally funded and has a DBE goal of 23%. The
Engineer’s Estimate is $23,896,800.75. This project is subject to the
Community Benefits Agreement (CBA).

Event Note The optional Pre-Bid Meeting will be held virtually via Microsoft Teams on
August 27, 2024, from 2:00 to 4:00 PM PST/PDT. Potential Bidders and
subcontractors are highly encouraged to attend. It is an opportunity to learn
about the Project, ask questions, and network with firms with whom you may
form a team for the Project. A copy of the presentation and the attendee list
will be published following the meeting on BidNet Direct for your reference to
assist potential Bidders and subcontractors connect. This posting is not an
endorsement by SANDAG of any of the individuals or firms listed. To join the
meeting, copoy and paste the link below into your browser.

Thank you for your interest in working with SANDAG. Please be sure to review and comply with all specifications and requirements.
Reminder: All Disadvantaged Business Enterprise (DBE) and Small Business (SB) firms are encouraged to apply for the SANDAG Bench
Program if your firm provides Architecture and Engineering (A&E), Construction Management (CM), and/or Planning Services. The Bench is
a pool of certified DBE and SB firms that Prime Consultants are able to access for work on SANDAG A&E, CM, and Planning On-Call
Contracts.
